blocks.text: The workshop is quiet, save for the frantic TINK of tiny tools and the rhythmic TICK-TOCK of a prominent GRANDFATHER CLOCK in the background.

ELIAS THORNE (70s, sharp, intense eyes) is hunched over his workbench, a jeweler’s loupe pressed to his eye. His brow is furrowed with deep concentration. A broken POCKET WATCH, its intricate gears exposed, lies before him.
His aged, yet steady, hands hold delicate TWEEZERS, trying to coax a TINY GEAR into place. It resists, a stubborn speck of metal.
A quick, anxious glance up at the grandfather clock. The time reads ELEVEN FIFTY-EIGHT. Two minutes to midnight.
Elias exhales slowly, carefully repositions his tweezers. His hands, usually so precise, show a faint tremor from the pressure.

character_wardrobe_notes.wardrobe: A worn but clean linen shirt, sleeves rolled to the elbows, a practical leather apron covering his chest and waist. His glasses perch on his nose.

scene_end.emotional_shift: Intense frustration and urgency shift to a glimmer of concentrated hope.
scene_end.turn: Elias makes a final, daring adjustment, and the watch mechanism gives a faint, hopeful twitch.

scenes.summary: Elias, an elderly watchmaker, works feverishly under the dim light of his desk lamp, struggling with a stubborn component in a broken pocket watch. The looming grandfather clock in the background shows only minutes remaining until midnight, intensifying his urgency.

blocks.text: Elias holds his breath, his concentration absolute. He nudges the tiny gear one last, infinitesimal fraction.
A soft, satisfying CLICK.
Simultaneously, the GRANDFATHER CLOCK begins to CHIME, a deep, resonant tone filling the workshop.
The pocket watch mechanism WHIRS to life. The hands, previously frozen, sweep forward. The minute hand aligns perfectly with the TWELVE, just as the first chime finishes.
Elias’s eyes widen slightly. Success.

character_wardrobe_notes.wardrobe: A worn but clean linen shirt, sleeves rolled to the elbows, a practical leather apron covering his chest and waist. His glasses perch on his nose.

scene_end.emotional_shift: Extreme tension and uncertainty resolve into immense relief and a quiet, building triumph.
scene_end.turn: The pocket watch ticks powerfully in Elias's hand, its new movement synchronized with the chiming grandfather clock.

scenes.summary: As the grandfather clock begins its resonant midnight chime, Elias makes a final, expert adjustment. The tiny gear clicks into place, and the pocket watch springs to life, its hands beginning to move, perfectly synchronized with the first stroke of midnight.

blocks.text: Elias gently holds the now-working POCKET WATCH, listening to its steady tick-tock. A tiny world, restored.
His gaze drifts up to the GRANDFATHER CLOCK, which finishes its final, lingering chime of midnight.
A slow, profound smile spreads across Elias’s face. Not a grin, but a quiet, deep satisfaction. The work is done.
He carefully closes the pocket watch, a silent acknowledgment of a battle won against time itself.

character_wardrobe_notes.wardrobe: A worn but clean linen shirt, sleeves rolled to the elbows, a practical leather apron covering his chest and waist. His glasses perch on his nose.

scene_end.emotional_shift: Profound relief settles into quiet pride and resolution.
scene_end.turn: Elias allows himself a rare moment of serene satisfaction, his urgent task successfully completed.

scenes.summary: Elias looks from the newly ticking pocket watch to the chiming grandfather clock, a subtle but profound expression of relief and quiet triumph on his face as the last chime of midnight fades.

blocks.text: The workshop is quiet, save for the frantic TINK of tiny tools and the rhythmic TICK-TOCK of a prominent GRANDFATHER CLOCK in the background.

ELIAS THORNE (70s, sharp, intense eyes) is hunched over his workbench, a jeweler’s loupe pressed to his eye. His brow is furrowed with deep concentration. A broken POCKET WATCH, its intricate gears exposed, lies before him.
His aged, yet steady, hands hold delicate TWEEZERS, trying to coax a TINY GEAR into place. It resists, a stubborn speck of metal.
A quick, anxious glance up at the grandfather clock. The time reads ELEVEN FIFTY-EIGHT. Two minutes to midnight.
Elias exhales slowly, carefully repositions his tweezers. His hands, usually so precise, show a faint tremor from the pressure.

scene_end.turn: Elias makes a final, daring adjustment, and the watch mechanism gives a faint, hopeful twitch.

scenes.summary: Elias, an elderly watchmaker, works feverishly under the dim light of his desk lamp, struggling with a stubborn component in a broken pocket watch. The looming grandfather clock in the background shows only minutes remaining until midnight, intensifying his urgency.

blocks.text: Elias holds his breath, his concentration absolute. He nudges the tiny gear one last, infinitesimal fraction.
A soft, satisfying CLICK.
Simultaneously, the GRANDFATHER CLOCK begins to CHIME, a deep, resonant tone filling the workshop.
The pocket watch mechanism WHIRS to life. The hands, previously frozen, sweep forward. The minute hand aligns perfectly with the TWELVE, just as the first chime finishes.
Elias’s eyes widen slightly. Success.

character_wardrobe_notes.wardrobe: A worn but clean linen shirt, sleeves rolled to the elbows, a practical leather apron covering his chest and waist. His glasses perch on his nose.

scene_end.emotional_shift: Extreme tension and uncertainty resolve into immense relief and a quiet, building triumph.
scene_end.turn: The pocket watch ticks powerfully in Elias's hand, its new movement synchronized with the chiming grandfather clock.

scenes.summary: As the grandfather clock begins its resonant midnight chime, Elias makes a final, expert adjustment. The tiny gear clicks into place, and the pocket watch springs to life, its hands beginning to move, perfectly synchronized with the first stroke of midnight.

blocks.text: Elias gently holds the now-working POCKET WATCH, listening to its steady tick-tock. A tiny world, restored.
His gaze drifts up to the GRANDFATHER CLOCK, which finishes its final, lingering chime of midnight.
A slow, profound smile spreads across Elias’s face. Not a grin, but a quiet, deep satisfaction. The work is done.
He carefully closes the pocket watch, a silent acknowledgment of a battle won against time itself.
